regions.name: Buenos Aires
regions.stay_type: design hotel in Palermo or San Telmo

activities.category: Tango milonga — watching and learning
Palermo food and wine neighbourhood walk
Recoleta Cemetery and literary café circuit
Day trip to a working estancia for asado

regions.name: Mendoza wine country
regions.stay_type: boutique wine lodge in the Luján de Cuyo or Uco Valley

activities.category: Malbec tasting at a family-owned bodega
Andes foothills horseback ride with Aconcagua views
Olive oil and food pairing at an artisan producer
Circuito Chico lakeside drive and hike
Full-day trekking in Nahuel Huapi National Park
Craft beer and chocolate tasting in Bariloche

common_mistakes: Treating Buenos Aires as a transit hub — four nights is the minimum to feel the city's rhythm; most people want a fifth after the fact
Visiting Mendoza in January or February when harvest hasn't started and temperatures hit 38°C — March to May is harvest season and the real experience
Booking Bariloche in ski season (July–August) if your goal is hiking — the trails are snow-covered and the town is packed with domestic ski tourists
Not pre-arranging bodega visits in Mendoza — the private-label producers that give the best experiences don't accept walk-ins

activities.category: Welcome asado and city overview walk
MALBA modern art and San Telmo market
Southern right whale watching (seasonal June–December)
Magellanic penguin colony at Punta Tombo
Orca hunting behaviour observation (seasonal)
Glacier trekking with crampons on Perito Moreno
Boat safari along the glacier face
Estancia horseback ride in steppe landscape

regions.name: El Chaltén (Fitz Roy massif)
regions.stay_type: mountain hostel-lodge at the edge of Los Glaciares

activities.category: Laguna de los Tres — Fitz Roy full-day hike
Cerro Torre viewpoint trail
Patagonian steppe birding walk

common_mistakes: Underestimating Patagonian weather — Fitz Roy hikes require a 5-day weather window; plan at least 3 nights in El Chaltén to catch one good day
Visiting Peninsula Valdés outside whale season (June–December) expecting the signature wildlife experience
Trying to combine El Chaltén and El Calafate in less than 5 days — the road between them is 3 hours and you need time in each
Booking El Chaltén accommodation in high season (January–February) less than 4 months ahead — the town has limited beds

regions.name: Salta
regions.stay_type: colonial boutique hotel in the historic centre

activities.category: Quebrada de Cafayate wine and rock-formation drive — torrontés tasting en route
Cachi and the Calchaquí Valleys high-altitude circuit
Peña folklore show and local gastronomy — locro, empanadas, humitas

regions.name: Jujuy (Quebrada de Humahuaca)
regions.stay_type: boutique adobe posada in Tilcara or Purmamarca

activities.category: Cerro de los Siete Colores at Purmamarca — UNESCO coloured mountain at dawn
Tilcara Pucará fortress and pre-Inca archaeological site
Salinas Grandes salt flats on the Altiplano — photography at 3,400 m

regions.name: Misiones — Iguazú Falls
regions.stay_type: lodge inside or adjacent to the national park

activities.category: Argentine side full walkway — Devil's Throat at opening hour before the crowd
Brazilian side panoramic walkway for the wide view (day trip or cross-border stay)
Jungle boat safari under the falls — expect to be completely soaked

common_mistakes: Combining Salta and Jujuy as a single overnight each — this is a road-trip region that rewards three nights in each to access the remote valleys
Arriving in Purmamarca after 10 am when tour buses have already packed the plaza — stay locally to see the mountain in morning silence
Visiting Iguazú only from the Argentine side — the Brazilian side gives the wide panoramic view; if you can cross for even half a day, do it
Scheduling this route in reverse (flying Iguazú first) — the northwest colonial cities are a better opening before the waterfall finale
